docs.text:
soma/
├── entropy.py             # CognitiveEntropyCalculator (~150 行)
├── entropy/
│   ├── __init__.py
│   └── negative_words.txt # 从零熵语料提取的负面词汇表（100-200词）

docs.title: 核心类设计
docs.text:
# soma/entropy.py

class CognitiveEntropyCalculator:
    """轻量级认知熵值计算器"""

    def __init__(self, window_size: int = 20):
        self.window_size = window_size
        self.negative_words = self._load_negative_words()

    def calculate(self, conversation_turns: List[dict]) -> dict:
        """
        返回:
        {
            "clarity_index": 72,           # 清朗指数 0-100
            "logic_score": 0.82,           # 逻辑连贯性
            "emotional_score": 0.75,       # 情绪稳定性
            "focus_score": 0.68,           # 目标聚焦度
            "grade": "安全区",              # 高风险区/安全区/高能区
            "trend": "improving",           # improving/stable/declining
            "suggestion": None,            # 若连续3轮<50，填充切换建议
        }
        """

    def _logic_coherence(self, turns: List[dict]) -> float:
        """语义片段间连贯性 —— 复用 embedder 计算相邻轮次余弦相似度"""

    def _emotional_stability(self, turns: List[dict]) -> float:
        """情绪扰动指数 —— negative_words 词表匹配"""

    def _focus_clarity(self, turns: List[dict]) -> float:
        """目标聚焦度 —— 话题一致性（连续轮次的主题向量方差）"""

    def _load_negative_words(self) -> set:
        """从零熵语料提取的高频功能词 100-200 个"""

docs.title: 与管道的集成
docs.text:
# SOMA_Agent 新增方法

def get_cognitive_state(self, user_id: str = "") -> dict:
    """获取当前用户的认知熵值状态"""
    recent_turns = self.analytics.get_recent_sessions(user_id, limit=20)
    return self.entropy_calculator.calculate(recent_turns)

docs.title: 负面词汇表设计（从零熵语料提取）
docs.text:
从零熵书院日常课程/复盘文本中提取高频功能词，按类别组织：

类别 示例词（各10-20个） 焦虑/不安 焦虑、烦躁、不安、担忧、恐慌、着急、紧张、心慌 迷茫/困惑 迷茫、困惑、不知道、不确定、混乱、模糊、搞不清楚 无力/疲惫 累、疲惫、无力、不想动、崩溃、撑不住、倦怠 情绪波动 烦、生气、恼怒、委屈、难过、伤心、低落、压抑 外归因 别人、领导、环境、市场、政策、没办法、不得已 犹豫/反复 纠结、犹豫、徘徊、反反复复、拿不准、下不了决心

  • 修改文件: 2 个（agent.py +10行，analytics.py 扩展 entropy 列）
  • 测试: ~12 项（三个维度的计算、边界值、趋势判断、词表覆盖）
  • 风险: 极低。完全独立的观察者模块，不修改管道逻辑
  • 工期: 约 1 周（核心计算 3 天 + 词表提取 1 天 + Dash 面板 2 天）

docs.title: 模块四：空性偏差检测升级
能力描述
docs.text: 在现有偏差检测基础上，新增「框架锚定识别」——检测用户是否过度锁定单一分析框架（如总从外部归因），并温和提示切换到其他视角。这体现了「空性」智慧：任何判断都依赖于特定框架，看见框架本身就是拓展认知边界。

docs.title: 现有基础
docs.text: 资产 位置 用途 ConflictDetector hub/_conflict.py 现有的记忆冲突检测，扩展点清晰 ActivationHub.anti_confirmation_search() hub/_core.py 已有反视角检索，新的偏差类型可在此追加 ActivationHub.activate() hub/_core.py 偏差检测结果从这传入 prompt _build_prompt() agent.py 已有「反面视角与潜在矛盾」section，可扩展

docs.title: 核心类设计
docs.text:
# soma/hub/_frame_detector.py

class FrameAnchoringDetector:
    """检测用户是否过度锁定单一分析框架"""

    def __init__(self, keywords_config: Path = None):
        self.frames = self._load_frames(keywords_config)
        self.window_size = 5  # 最近 N 轮对话

    def detect(self, conversation_turns: List[dict]) -> Optional[dict]:
        """
        返回:
        None — 无框架锚定
        {
            "frame": "外部归因",
            "ratio": 0.75,
            "opposite_frame": "内在归因",
            "reflection": "我注意到您在过去几次对话中，有75%的分析都采用了「外部归因」框架..."
        }
        """

    def _load_frames(self, config_path) -> dict:
        """加载框架关键词库 {框架名: [关键词列表]}"""

    def _count_frame_usage(self, turns) -> dict:
        """统计各框架在最近 N 轮中的使用频率"""

frames:
  外部归因:

docs.title: 模块五：商道罗盘与企熵诊断仪
能力描述
docs.text: 以插件形式提供两个可选的场景化功能模块。每个插件包含自己的触发器、对话状态机与数据记录，独立于核心推理管道运行。

docs.title: 插件基类设计
docs.text:
# soma/plugin_base.py

from abc import ABC, abstractmethod

class SomaPlugin(ABC):
    """SOMA 功能插件基类"""

    plugin_id: str
    plugin_name: str
    description: str

    @abstractmethod
    def should_activate(self, user_input: str, context: dict) -> bool:
        """判断是否应激活此插件"""

    @abstractmethod
    def run(self, agent: "SOMA_Agent", user_input: str, context: dict) -> dict:
        """执行插件对话流，返回结果"""

    @abstractmethod
    def get_state(self) -> dict:
        """获取当前会话状态（供持久化）"""


class PluginRegistry:
    """插件注册表"""

    def discover(self): ...
    def register(self, plugin: SomaPlugin): ...
    def find_active(self, user_input: str, context: dict) -> List[SomaPlugin]: ...

docs.title: 插件接入 agent.py
docs.text:
# SOMA_Agent.respond() 入口新增

def respond(self, problem: str, user_id: str = "") -> str:
    # 检查是否有应激活的插件
    active_plugins = self.plugin_registry.find_active(
        problem, {"user_id": user_id, "user_profile": self._get_profile(user_id)}
    )

    if active_plugins:
        # 插件拦截模式：由插件接管对话
        for plugin in active_plugins:
            result = plugin.run(self, problem, context)
            if result.get("handled"):
                return result["response"]

    # 否则进入正常推理管道
    ...

docs.text:
{
    "$schema": "https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema",
    "title": "SOMA Pattern Contribution",
    "type": "object",
    "required": ["pattern_name", "description", "category", "contributor"],
    "properties": {
        "pattern_name": {
            "type": "string",

        },

            "maxLength": 500
        },
        "trigger_conditions": {
            "type": "array",
            "items": {
                "type": "object",
                "properties": {

                    "semantic_description": {"type": "string"}
                }
            }
        },
        "processing_flow": {
            "type": "array",
            "items": {
                "type": "object",

                    "step": {"type": "string"},
                    "prompt": {"type": "string"},
                    "inputs": {"type": "array", "items": {"type": "string"}},
                    "outputs": {"type": "array", "items": {"type": "string"}}
                }
            }
        },
        "effect_metrics": {
            "type": "array",
            "items": {"type": "string"},
            "description": "衡量该规律效果的指标"
        },
        "category": {
            "type": "string",
            "enum": ["家庭关系", "个人成长", "商业实践", "团队协作", "身心健康", "学习方法", "其他"]
        },
        "contributor": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "贡献者的零熵 ID"
        },
        "empirical_basis": {
            "type": "string",

            "maxLength": 300
        }
    }
}

docs.title: 核心类设计
docs.text:
# soma/contribution.py

class PatternSubmission:
    """用户提交的规律"""
    pattern_name: str
    description: str
    category: str
    contributor: str
    trigger_conditions: List[dict]
    processing_flow: List[dict]
    effect_metrics: List[str]
    empirical_basis: str

    # 审核状态
    status: str = "pending"  # pending / auto_reviewed / approved / rejected
    energy_score: int = 0     # 被采纳能量值
    embedding: Optional[np.ndarray] = None


class ContributionHandler:
    """规律贡献处理器"""

    def __init__(self, engine: WisdomEngine, embedder, store: ContributionStore):
        self.engine = engine
        self.embedder = embedder
        self.store = store

    def submit(self, submission: PatternSubmission) -> str:
        """提交新规律，返回 submission_id"""
        # 1. 格式校验 (JSON Schema)
        # 2. 计算 embedding
        # 3. 与现有规律做去重检查（相似度 < 0.85 才通过）
        # 4. 持久化到 contribution_store
        # 5. 返回 submission_id 供跟踪

    def auto_review(self, submission_id: str) -> dict:
        """自动第一轮审核"""
        # 格式完整性 + 与已有规律去重 + 触发条件合理性

    def manual_approve(self, submission_id: str, reviewer_id: str) -> bool:
        """人工审核通过，纳入规律网络"""
        # 生成 pattern_id，加入 wisdom_laws.yaml 和向量索引

    def record_adoption(self, pattern_id: str, outcome: str):
        """记录规律被采纳的结果"""
        # 成功 → energy_score +1
        # 负面反馈 → energy_score -2
        # 低于阈值 → 自动降级，不再参与检索

    def get_active_patterns(self, context: dict, limit: int = 5) -> list:
        """检索当前上下文应激活的贡献规律"""
        # 基于语义匹配 + energy_score 阈值过滤

docs.text:
用户提交
    │
    ▼
┌──────────────┐
│ 自动审核       │ ← JSON Schema 校验 + embedding 去重（sim < 0.85）
│ (秒级完成)     │
└──────┬───────┘
       │
       ▼
┌──────────────┐
│ 人工审核       │ ← 零熵书院认证「规律审核员」手动审查
│ (文化+技术)    │    检查价值观合规性 + 实用性
└──────┬───────┘
       │
       ▼
┌──────────────┐
│ 纳入规律网络   │ ← 生成 pattern_id，加入索引
│               │    初始 energy_score = 5
└──────┬───────┘
       │
       ▼
┌──────────────┐
│ 运行中追踪     │ ← 每采纳一次 energy_score +1
│               │    负面反馈 energy_score -2
│               │    score < 0 → 自动降级
└──────────────┘

docs.title: 总代码量估算
docs.text:
类别 代码量 新增代码 ~1,800 行 修改代码 ~140 行 测试代码 ~600 行 配置文件 ~200 行 YAML 总计 ~2,740 行

相比 v0.9.0 新增 ~1,200 行，v0.10.0 的改动量约为 v0.9.0 的 1.5 倍。

docs.title: 技术风险
docs.text: 风险 等级 缓解措施 钩子注入影响管道性能 低 钩子只做字符串模板填充，O(1)；可配置开关 负面词汇表覆盖不足 中 先用零熵语料初始化 150 词，开放热更新接口 中道算法的「熵变估算」过于主观 中 第一个版本用简单的代理指标（连贯性+关键词），后续迭代 插件拦截错误导致正常管道不可用 中 插件运行在 try/except 中，异常时自动回退正常管道 规律贡献的审核瓶颈 中 自动审核负责去重和格式，人工只审价值观和实用性

docs.title: 待讨论决策问题
docs.text:
以下问题建议与文化赋能师和开发者一起讨论后确定：

D1 — 钩子的侵入程度：钩子提示是仅记录在日志中（静默），还是实时呈现给用户？如果实时呈现，以什么形式（附加在回答末尾 / 单独提示 / 仅对开发者可见）？

D2 — 认知熵值的「觉察引导模式」触发阈值：连续几轮熵值低于多少时切换？50 分？40 分？切换后如何恢复？

D3 — 中道算法的「阴阳权重」初始值：行动力(阳)和觉知力(阴)各 0.5 是否合理？不同用户类型是否需要不同初始配置？

D4 — 规律贡献的价值观边界：什么算「善」的规律？是否需要一份明确的价值观审核标准文档？

D5 — 贡献规律的版本管理：当原始贡献者修改一个已被广泛采纳的规律时，如何处理——更新还是分叉？

D6 — 零熵测试系统的集成程度：认知熵值和零熵现有的「能量信息测试系统」之间是什么关系——独立并行、数据互相参照、还是深度集成？

docs.title: 总结
docs.text:
六个模块的整合不是向 SOMA「添加功能」，而是为其注入一种全新的认知范式。当前 SOMA 是一个高效的问题解决引擎——它能思考、能记忆、能进化。整合零熵智慧后，SOMA 将增加一个新的维度：觉察。

它不只帮用户「想得更清楚」，还能帮用户「看见自己在怎么想」。

技术上，六个模块的设计遵循了同一个约束：每个模块都可以独立开关、独立迭代、独立验证。没有一个是必须依赖其他模块才能运行的。这让实施可以灵活推进，有任何模块达不到预期都可以单独回退而不影响整体。

文档版本: 1.0 | 适用于: SOMA v0.9.0 → v0.10.0 规划 | 生成日期: 2026-05-12

docs.title: SOMA v0.9.0 Comprehensive Capability Introduction
docs.text: From "Single-Agent Reasoning" to "Multi-Agent Collaboration" — a paradigm shift

docs.title: Table of Contents
docs.text:
1. Version Overview
  2. Core Architecture
  3. Five Core Capabilities
  4. Technical Details
  5. Benchmarks
  6. Significance for Different Roles
  7. Quick Start
  8. Complete API Reference

docs.title: Version Overview
docs.text: SOMA v0.9.0 is the largest architectural upgrade since v0.1.0. While v0.8.0 completed the individual agent's cognitive reasoning loop (causal reasoning, conflict detection, cross-domain analogy, quality evaluation), v0.9.0 achieves the leap from individual to collective intelligence — enabling multiple SOMA Agents to collaborate as a team, each with independent memory, independent evolution paths, and specialized expertise domains, forming collective intelligence that surpasses any individual through expert routing, distributed evolution, and consensus protocols.

docs.title: Evolution Roadmap
docs.text:
v0.5.0 — Foundation: 7 Thinking Laws + Vector Retrieval + Evolution Loop
v0.6.0 — Reasoning Templates: Structured Reasoning Framework + Benchmark System
v0.7.0 — Memory Intelligence: Consolidation + Active Forgetting + External Knowledge Import
v0.8.0 — Cognitive Reasoning: Causal Chains + Conflict Detection + Cross-Domain Analogy + Quality Evaluation
v0.9.0 — Collective Intelligence: Multi-Agent Collaboration + Expert Routing + Distributed Evolution + Consensus ← Current

docs.title: Key Numbers
docs.text: Metric Value Test Cases 473 all passing Multi-Agent Modules 4 new modules (registry / router / evolve / consensus) New Code ~1,200 lines External Dependencies Added 0 (all built on existing stack) Backward Compatibility 100% (all v0.8.0 APIs unchanged)

docs.title: 1. AgentRegistry
docs.text:
Formalizing Domain Expertise

Each Agent is no longer a homogeneous "general thinker" — instead, each is a specialized intelligent entity with explicit expertise domain labels, success rate tracking, and session statistics.

from soma.multi_agent import AgentRegistry, AgentInfo

registry = AgentRegistry()

# Register a legal expert agent
registry.register(
    agent=law_agent,
    expertise=["legal", "contracts", "litigation", "intellectual property"],
    description="Specializes in legal text analysis, contract review, and compliance assessment",
    is_default=False,
)

# Register a technical architect agent
registry.register(
    agent=tech_agent,
    expertise=["technology", "architecture", "performance optimization", "system design"],
    description="Specializes in distributed systems architecture, performance bottleneck analysis, and technology selection",
)

# Find experts by domain
experts = registry.find_experts("legal")
# → [(law_agent, 1.0), ...]

# Get all agent statistics
for info in registry.list_agents():
    print(f"{info.agent_id}: {info.expertise} "
          f"(sessions={info.session_count}, sr={info.success_rate:.2f})")

Key Features:

  • Zero external dependencies: pure in-memory dict + dataclass, no Redis/ZooKeeper needed
  • Expertise matching algorithm: exact match 1.0 → fuzzy match 0.7 → no match 0.0
  • Session statistics: automatically tracks each agent's session count and success rate, providing weighted basis for distributed evolution
  • Default fallback: supports setting a general-purpose agent as fallback

docs.title: 2. ExpertRouter
docs.text:
"The right person for the right question"

A three-tier routing strategy ensuring each question reaches the most suitable expert agent. No LLM involvement in routing decisions — zero API calls, zero latency.

User Question
    │
    ▼
┌──────────────┐
│ L1: Keyword   │ ← 8 domains × 80+ keywords, millisecond-level
│   Match       │   Legal / Tech / Finance / Management / Product
│   Hit → Route │   Science / Education / Healthcare
└──────┬───────┘
       │ No match
       ▼
┌──────────────┐
│ L2: Semantic  │ ← Requires embedder, computes cosine similarity
│   Match       │   between question and expertise descriptions
│   sim≥0.3 → Route │
└──────┬───────┘
       │ No match
       ▼
┌──────────────┐
│ L3: Default   │ ← General-purpose agent as fallback
│   Fallback    │
└──────────────┘

L1 Domain Keyword Table:

Domain Example Keywords Legal law, contract, litigation, regulation, compliance, IP, copyright, patent, arbitration Technology code, architecture, API, database, deploy, algorithm, performance, bug, programming Finance investment, stock, fund, budget, cost, revenue, profit, tax Management team, leadership, organization, strategy, process, performance review, hiring, culture Product user, requirements, experience, design, feature, iteration, growth, retention, conversion Science experiment, hypothesis, data, statistics, variable, control group, significance, correlation Education learning, teaching, curriculum, training, knowledge, skill, exam Healthcare diagnosis, treatment, medication, surgery, patient, clinical, pathology, prevention 
from soma.multi_agent import ExpertRouter

router = ExpertRouter(registry, embedder=embedder)

# Single-expert routing
agent, confidence, strategy = router.route(
    "How to review IP clauses in a SaaS contract?"
)
# → (law_agent, 0.67, "l1")  # Hit "legal"+"contract"+"IP" keywords

# Multi-expert routing (for multi-angle analysis)
agents = router.route_multi("Strategic planning for digital transformation", top_k=3)
# → [(tech_agent, 0.67, "l1"), (mgmt_agent, 0.67, "l1"), (product_agent, 0.33, "l1")]

# Routing statistics
print(router.stats)
# → {"route_count": 100, "l1_hit_rate": 0.73, "l2_hit_rate": 0.15, "fallback_rate": 0.12}

docs.title: 3. DistributedEvolver
docs.text:
Each Agent evolves independently, periodically merged into global wisdom

This is the most ambitious capability of v0.9.0. In traditional single-agent scenarios, the weights of 7 thinking laws could only be adjusted through a single agent's reflection. In v0.9.0, each expert agent maintains its own MetaEvolver instance (independent evolver_<agent_id>.db), evolving weights from their respective domain experience, then aggregating into global weights through merge strategies.

  Agent A (Legal)       Agent B (Tech)        Agent C (Management)
  Independent           Independent           Independent
  Evolution             Evolution             Evolution
  evolver_A.db          evolver_B.db          evolver_C.db
      │                     │                     │
      └─────────────────────┼─────────────────────┘
                            │
                    ┌───────▼───────┐
                    │  Global Merge │
                    │  3 Strategies │
                    └───────┬───────┘
                            │
                    ┌───────▼───────┐
                    │  Conflict     │
                    │  Arbitration  │
                    │  Divergence    │
                    │  >0.15: favor │
                    │  higher SR    │
                    └───────────────┘

Three Merge Strategies:

Strategy Formula Use Case simple_average Equal-weight average Homogeneous agent teams weighted_by_sessions Weighted by session count More experienced agents have more say weighted_by_success Weighted by success rate Recommended default — more effective agents have more say

Conflict Arbitration Mechanism:

When two agents adjust the same law's weight in opposite directions (one up, one down) and the divergence exceeds 0.15, arbitration is automatically triggered — favoring the agent with higher success rate, pulling toward the best weight by 60%.

from soma.multi_agent import DistributedEvolver
from pathlib import Path

evo = DistributedEvolver(
    persist_dir=Path("./multi_agent_data"),
    default_weights={"first_principles": 0.9, "systems_thinking": 1.0, ...},
    merge_strategy="weighted_by_success",
)

# Register each agent's evolver
evo.register_agent("law_agent", law_agent.evolver, session_count=50, success_rate=0.85)
evo.register_agent("tech_agent", tech_agent.evolver, session_count=120, success_rate=0.92)
evo.register_agent("mgmt_agent", mgmt_agent.evolver, session_count=30, success_rate=0.78)

# Trigger all agents to evolve independently
changes = evo.evolve_all()
# → {"law_agent": [{"type": "weight_adjustment", ...}], ...}

# Merge into global weights
global_weights = evo.merge_weights()
# → {"first_principles": 0.91, "systems_thinking": 0.97, ...}

# Apply global weights to all agents
applied = evo.apply_all()
# → 3

# Statistics
print(evo.get_stats())
# → {"agent_count": 3, "merge_count": 1, "merge_strategy": "weighted_by_success", ...}

docs.title: 4. ConsensusProtocol
docs.text:
Enabling multiple Agents to reach consensus on the same question

When a complex problem requires multiple experts to collectively assess from different angles, the consensus protocol provides three-tier strategies to coordinate disagreements and produce a unified conclusion.

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              ConsensusProtocol                       │
│                                                      │
│  Input: Question + multiple AgentOpinions             │
│                                                      │
│  ┌──────────────┐                                    │
│  │ Auto-detect   │                                    │
│  │ agreement     │                                    │
│  │ level         │                                    │
│  │ ≥0.8 → Strong │                                    │
│  │ 0.5-0.8 →     │                                    │
│  │ Moderate       │                                    │
│  │ <0.5 → Severe  │                                    │
│  └──────┬───────┘                                    │
│         │                                            │
│  ┌──────▼──────────────────────────────────────┐     │
│  │ L1: Weighted Voting                          │     │
│  │   confidence × answer aggregation            │     │
│  │   For: simple questions, many opinions        │     │
│  ├──────────────────────────────────────────────┤     │
│  │ L2: LLM Arbitration                          │     │
│  │   LLM reads all opinions, resolves disputes  │     │
│  │   For: moderate disagreement, external view  │     │
│  ├──────────────────────────────────────────────┤     │
│  │ L3: Dialectical Synthesis                    │     │
│  │   Thesis → Antithesis → Synthesis            │     │
│  │   For: deep philosophical questions,         │     │
│  │   irreconcilable contradictions              │     │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────┘     │
│                                                      │
│  Output: ConsensusResult                              │
│    · consensus_answer: final unified answer           │
│    · agreement_level: consensus degree 0-1            │
│    · minority_view: minority opinion (if any)         │
│    · disagreements: detailed list of disagreement     │
│      points                                          │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

from soma.multi_agent import ConsensusProtocol, AgentOpinion

protocol = ConsensusProtocol(llm_call=my_llm_function)

# Each agent produces their opinion
opinions = [
    AgentOpinion(
        agent_id="law_agent",
        answer="From a legal perspective, the main risk in this contract is the IP ownership clause...",
        confidence=0.85,
        key_points=["Unclear IP ownership", "Asymmetric liability", "Disputed jurisdiction"],
    ),
    AgentOpinion(
        agent_id="tech_agent",
        answer="Technically, we need to focus on delivery standards and support terms...",
        confidence=0.78,
        key_points=["Vague acceptance criteria", "Undefined support SLA"],
    ),
    AgentOpinion(
        agent_id="mgmt_agent",
        answer="From a management perspective, the key risks are delivery timeline and change management...",
        confidence=0.72,
        key_points=["Unreasonable delivery timeline", "Missing change management process"],
    ),
]

# Form consensus
result = protocol.form_consensus("Review the comprehensive risks of this contract", opinions)

print(f"Agreement level: {result.agreement_level:.2f}")
print(f"Strategy: {result.strategy_used}")
# → strategy_used: "voting" (low divergence), "llm_arbitration" (moderate), "dialectical_synthesis" (severe)

if result.is_strong_consensus:
    print("Strong consensus reached!")
else:
    print(f"{len(result.disagreements)} disagreement points found")
    if result.minority_view:
        print(f"Minority view: {result.minority_view}")

Agreement Level Detection Algorithm:

  1. Extract core claims (key_points) from all agent answers
  2. Compute Jaccard similarity of claim sets
  3. Weight by confidence (higher confidence agents have more weight)
  4. Output agreement_level ∈ [0, 1]

docs.title: 5. Memory Isolation
docs.text:
Three-State Isolation Model: Private / Group-Shared / Global

v0.9.0 adds agent_id and shared_group_id fields to MemoryUnit, enabling finer-grained memory access control.

┌────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              Memory Isolation               │
│                                             │
│  ┌──────────┐  ┌──────────┐  ┌──────────┐  │
│  │ Agent A  │  │ Agent B  │  │ Agent C  │  │
│  │ Private  │  │ Private  │  │ Private  │  │
│  │ agent=A  │  │ agent=B  │  │ agent=C  │  │
│  └────┬─────┘  └────┬─────┘  └────┬─────┘  │
│       │              │              │       │
│       └──────────────┼──────────────┘       │
│                      │                      │
│              ┌───────▼───────┐              │
│              │ Group-Shared  │              │
│              │ group="team"  │              │
│              │ A,B,C visible │              │
│              └───────────────┘              │
│                                             │
│           ┌──────────────────┐              │
│           │   Global Memory   │              │
│           │   agent=""        │              │
│           │   all agents      │              │
│           └──────────────────┘              │
└────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Isolation Enforcement in Query Paths:

  • FTS5 Keyword Search Path: SQL WHERE clause precisely filters agent_id = ? OR shared_group_id = ?
  • Vector Semantic Search Path: Python in-memory filtering mem.agent_id != agent_id → skip
  • Semantic Memory: namespace-encoded isolation (agent_id or user_id)
  • Skill Memory: user_id + agent/group dual-channel filtering

Vector Index Startup Health Check (new in v0.9.0):

When vector search is enabled after the fact (historical memories lack vectors), startup automatically detects indexed/total < 50% and triggers a full rebuild. This solves the upgrade scenario where "memories exist, but vectors don't" — causing semantic search to degrade to keyword search.

docs.title: Zero External Dependencies
docs.text:
All 4 new v0.9.0 modules are built entirely on SOMA's existing tech stack:

Component Storage Computation Communication AgentRegistry Python dict O(1) lookup In-process ExpertRouter Python dict O(n) keyword scan In-process DistributedEvolver SQLite (per agent) Weighted average In-process ConsensusProtocol No persistence Jaccard + LLM In-process

No Redis (registry center), RabbitMQ (message queue), gRPC (service communication), or etcd (configuration center) needed. All agents collaborate within a single Python process, suitable for:

  • Edge deployment (Raspberry Pi / embedded devices)
  • Single-machine multi-agent scenarios (customer service systems, education platforms)
  • Air-gapped environments (classified scenarios, offline terminals)

docs.title: Multi-Agent Test Suite
docs.text:
v0.9.0 adds 51 multi-agent specific tests (tests/test_multi_agent.py):

Module Test Count Coverage AgentRegistry 10 Register/unregister/find/default/dedup/stats ExpertRouter 9 L1 routing/L2 routing/fallback/multi-route/stats DistributedEvolver 15 Register/unregister/independent evolution/3 merge strategies/conflict arbitration/global apply ConsensusProtocol 13 Strong consensus/LLM arbitration/dialectical synthesis/disagreement detection/empty opinions Integration 4 End-to-end multi-agent collaboration flow

docs.title: Benchmarks
docs.text:
v0.9.0 achieved the following scores in the four-dimensional benchmark system (based on 1,752 real memories from 零熵智库 production):

Dimension Score Details Memory 89.4 Semantic recall rate 100%, dedup rate 100%, query latency 34ms Wisdom 81.1 Decomposition coverage 100%, thought diversity entropy 0.88, memory relevance 1.21 Evolution 75.0 200 reflections, 281 solidified skills, recent 30-session success rate 100% Scalability 100 1K→10K query latency linear growth log₂(N) Overall 84.9 —

docs.title: For AI Agent Developers
docs.text:
From "Building Tools" to "Building Teams"

Before v0.9.0, you used SOMA to equip a single agent with a thinking engine. After v0.9.0, you can create a team of expert agents — legal experts, technical advisors, management coaches — each with their own role, automatically assigned tasks through routing, and resolving disagreements through consensus protocols.

Real-World Scenarios:

  • Enterprise AI Middle Platform: One SOMA instance managing 40 domain-specific avatars, each with independent memory and evolution paths, sharing a global knowledge base
  • Intelligent Customer Service: Frontend router automatically dispatches by question type to technical/after-sales/complaint experts, no LLM needed for intent recognition
  • Education Tutoring Platform: Math expert, language expert, science expert evolve independently, periodically merge teaching strategy weights

What Doesn't Need to Change:

  • Existing soma.remember() / soma.respond() / soma.query_memory() APIs are completely unchanged
  • Single-agent usage is 100% compatible with v0.8.0
  • Only use soma.multi_agent module when you need multi-agent collaboration

docs.title: For Large Language Models (LLMs)
docs.text:
From "One Model Answers Everything" to "Expert Models for Each Domain"

v0.9.0's multi-agent architecture enables different LLM instances (or different prompt configurations of the same LLM) to play different roles:

  • Reduce Hallucinations: The consensus protocol's LLM arbitration layer cross-validates multiple independent judgments, reducing single-model bias
  • Save Tokens: The routing layer only sends questions to the most relevant Agent, rather than having all Agents participate
  • Break Through Single-Model Limitations: Different Agents can connect to different LLMs (Legal Agent using DeepSeek, Tech Agent using Claude, Management Agent using GPT), leveraging each model's strengths

Key Insight: v0.9.0 doesn't make LLMs stronger — it makes the organization of LLMs smarter.

docs.title: For End Users
docs.text:
From "Asking AI" to "Asking an Expert Team"

When an average user asks "I want to start a business — what legal risks should I watch out for?":

  • Traditional AI: Gives generic advice, may miss critical legal details
  • v0.8.0 SOMA: Decomposes the question through 7 thinking laws, provides systematic analysis combining memory
  • v0.9.0 SOMA: Router automatically identifies "startup" → management, "legal risks" → legal, simultaneously engages both management expert and legal expert — both Agents analyze independently, then synthesize final advice through the consensus protocol

Users don't need to know how many Agents are working behind the scenes — they just see a more comprehensive, more reliable answer that has been verified by multiple experts.

docs.title: For Technical Decision Makers
docs.text:
From "Choosing a Library" to "Choosing an Architecture"

SOMA is not another vector database, not another agent framework — it is a complete cognitive architecture:

Layer What SOMA Provides What Traditional Approaches Require Reasoning 7 thinking laws + reasoning templates Hand-written prompt engineering Memory SQLite + Vector + FTS5 + three stores Multi-component assembly (ChromaDB + PostgreSQL + Redis) Evolution Automatic weight adjustment + merge/forget None (or hand-written rules) Collaboration Registry + Router + Consensus Protocol ZooKeeper + Message Queue + hand-written protocols Deployment pip install soma-wisdom Docker Compose / K8s multi-service orchestration

Single binary startup, zero operational burden.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: pip install soma-wisdom>=0.9.0

docs.title: 5 Minutes: Create Your First Expert Team
docs.text:
from soma import SOMA, SOMA_Agent, SOMAConfig
from soma.multi_agent import (
    AgentRegistry, ExpertRouter,
    DistributedEvolver, ConsensusProtocol, AgentOpinion,
)
from pathlib import Path

# 1. Create 3 expert Agents
config = SOMAConfig(
    framework_path="wisdom_laws.yaml",
    episodic_persist_dir=Path("./team_data"),
    use_vector_search=True,
)

law_agent = SOMA_Agent(config, agent_id="law_expert", group_id="team")
tech_agent = SOMA_Agent(config, agent_id="tech_architect", group_id="team")
mgmt_agent = SOMA_Agent(config, agent_id="mgmt_coach", group_id="team")

# 2. Register in registry
registry = AgentRegistry()
registry.register(law_agent, expertise=["legal", "contract", "compliance", "IP"],
                  description="Legal risk analysis expert")
registry.register(tech_agent, expertise=["tech", "architecture", "performance", "security"],
                  description="Technical architecture advisor")
registry.register(mgmt_agent, expertise=["management", "strategy", "team", "organization"],
                  description="Management coach", is_default=True)

# 3. Create router
router = ExpertRouter(registry, embedder=law_agent.embedder)

# 4. Route question to expert
expert, confidence, strategy = router.route(
    "How to review IP clauses in a SaaS contract?"
)
print(f"Routed to: {expert.agent_id} (confidence={confidence:.2f}, strategy={strategy})")
# → Routed to: law_expert (confidence=0.67, strategy=l1)

# 5. Answer with the routed expert
answer = expert.respond(
    "How to review IP clauses in a SaaS contract?",
    user_id="user_001"
)
print(answer)

docs.title: 10 Minutes: Multi-Agent Consensus Decision
docs.text:
# Multiple experts give opinions on the same complex question
question = "Should the company build an in-house AI team or outsource?"

opinions = []
for agent_id in ["law_expert", "tech_architect", "mgmt_coach"]:
    agent = registry.get(agent_id)
    response = agent.respond(question, user_id="ceo_001")
    # Extract core claims from response (simplified example)
    foci = agent.decompose(question)
    key_points = [f.dimension[:60] for f in foci[:3]]

    opinions.append(AgentOpinion(
        agent_id=agent_id,
        answer=response,
        confidence=0.8,
        foci=foci,
        key_points=key_points,
    ))

# Form consensus
protocol = ConsensusProtocol()
result = protocol.form_consensus(question, opinions)

print(f"Agreement level: {result.agreement_level:.2f}")
print(f"Strategy: {result.strategy_used}")
print(f"Final opinion: {result.consensus_answer[:200]}")
if result.minority_view:
    print(f"Minority view: {result.minority_view}")

docs.title: AgentRegistry
docs.text: Method Description register(agent, expertise, description, is_default) Register an expert agent unregister(agent_id) Unregister an agent get(agent_id) Get agent instance get_default() Get default fallback agent find_experts(domain, min_score) Find matching experts by domain list_agents() List all agent metadata record_session(agent_id, success) Record session result, update stats agent_count Number of registered agents

docs.title: ExpertRouter
docs.text: Method Description route(problem, min_confidence, allow_fallback) Single-expert routing, returns (agent, confidence, strategy) route_multi(problem, top_k, min_confidence) Multi-expert routing, returns top-K matches stats Routing statistics: route_count, l1_hit_rate, l2_hit_rate, fallback_rate

docs.title: DistributedEvolver
docs.text: Method Description register_agent(agent_id, evolver, session_count, success_rate) Register agent evolver unregister_agent(agent_id) Unregister evolve_agent(agent_id) Trigger single agent evolution evolve_all() Trigger all agents' independent evolution merge_weights(strategy) Execute global weight merge (3 strategies available) get_global_weights() Get most recent merged global weights apply_global_weights(agent_id) Apply global weights to a specific agent apply_all() Apply global weights to all agents get_stats() Distributed evolution statistics

docs.title: ConsensusProtocol
docs.text: Method Description form_consensus(question, opinions) Form consensus, returns ConsensusResult ConsensusResult.is_strong_consensus Whether consensus is strong (≥0.8) ConsensusResult.agent_count Number of participating agents ConsensusResult.minority_view Minority opinion (if any)

docs.title: MemoryUnit (New Fields)
docs.text: Field Type Description agent_id str ID of the agent that owns this memory shared_group_id str ID of the collaboration group this memory belongs to user_id str ID of the user that owns this memory (existing)

docs.title: Production-Verified Configuration (零熵智库)
docs.text:
The following configuration has been validated in real production (40 avatars, 1,752 memories, 34 days continuous operation):

soma = SOMA(
    llm="openai/deepseek-chat",
    use_vector_search=True,
    recall_threshold=0.01,   # Low threshold, sorted by RRF fusion
    top_k=15,                 # Moderate candidate pool size
    persist_dir="/opt/soma_data",
)

docs.title: Performance Guidelines
docs.text:
• < 100 memories: No special optimization needed
  • 100 - 2,000 memories: Recommended config (current production experience)
  • > 2,000 memories: Consider raising recall_threshold, lowering top_k, increasing evolve() interval
  • Multi-Agent scenarios: Separate directories per agent to avoid SQLite lock contention

docs.title: Upgrading from v0.8.0
docs.text:
pip install --upgrade soma-wisdom>=0.9.0

Zero code changes — all v0.8.0 APIs are backward compatible. Multi-agent capabilities are provided through the new soma.multi_agent module and do not affect existing code.

On startup, if historical memories are detected to lack vector indices (indexed/total < 50%), a full rebuild is automatically triggered. The first startup may take 2-5 minutes (depending on memory count); subsequent startups complete instantly.

docs.title: Looking Ahead to v0.10.0
docs.text:
v0.9.0 completes the leap from individual to collective intelligence. Exploration directions for v0.10.0:

  • Agent Auto-Spawning: Automatically create new expert Agents when the router detects high fallback rates in certain domains
  • Cross-Agent Knowledge Distillation: Automatically transfer experience from high-success-rate Agents to low-success-rate Agents
  • Agent Market Protocol: Knowledge trading and reputation system between Agents
  • Federated Evolution: Securely share evolution weights across multiple SOMA instances (different servers) without sharing raw memory data

SOMA — Wisdom over Memory. Now, Wisdom over Solitude.

Document version: 1.0 | Applicable version: SOMA v0.9.0rc1 | Generated: 2026-05-12
